methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $4.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Saronic
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $9.25B

private.name: Epirus
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $1.35B

data.narrative:
Chaos Industries is a Series D defense and security company founded by John Tenet and Bo Marr, currently valued at $4.5B post-money with $1B+ raised to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies spanning both public and private markets: two public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private companies including Saronic ($9.2B Series D), Flock Safety ($7.5B Series E), Skydio ($2.2B Series E), Epirus ($1.4B Series D), and Hidden Level ($500M Series C).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $4.5B last post-money valuation and applying a typical Series D-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.5x, implies a valuation of $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across the five closest same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $2.2B, with a range spanning $500M to $9.2B. These two methods produce materially different signals, reflecting dispersion in the peer set and inherent uncertainty around appropriate multiplier sizing in this stage range.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a triangulated valuation range of $1.9B to $7.8B, with a median of $4.5B. The current $4.5B valuation aligns with the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiple for this market, given the wide peer valuation spread, and the relevance of public comps to this private cohort's fundamentals.
